headline: Musk says Tesla may 'tape out' next-generation AI6 chips in December
summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk said on Thursday that ‌the automaker may be able ‌to "tape out", a stage when a chip design ​is finalised and sent to a factory for production, its next-generation AI6 chips in December.  Musk said last year that ‌Samsung Electronics ⁠would make the AI6 chips, likely to be used in ⁠self-driving cars and humanoid robots at Tesla' new factory in Taylor, Texas, after ​the Korean ​company clinched ​a $16.5 billion deal ‌to supply artificial intelligence chips to the electric car maker.  A Samsung ​executive said ​on Wednesday that it plans to produce Tesla chips, based on Samsung's ​advanced ‌2-nanomete process, in the second half ​of 2027.

content.title: Musk says Tesla may 'tape out' next-generation AI6 chips in December
content.summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk said on Thursday that ‌the automaker may be able ‌to "tape out", a stage when a chip design ​is finalised and sent to a factory for production, its next-generation AI6 chips in December.  Musk said last year that ‌Samsung Electronics ⁠would make the AI6 chips, likely to be used in ⁠self-driving cars and humanoid robots at Tesla' new factory in Taylor, Texas, after ​the Korean ​company clinched ​a $16.5 billion deal ‌to supply artificial intelligence chips to the electric car maker.  A Samsung ​executive said ​on Wednesday that it plans to produce Tesla chips, based on Samsung's ​advanced ‌2-nanomete process, in the second half ​of 2027.

headline: New Brattle Report Finds Better Utilization of Existing Power Grid Could Save US Consumers More Than $100 Billion in the Next Decade
summary: According to new analysis from The Brattle Group, better utilization of the existing US power system could save consumers more than $100 billion over the next decade. Much of the nation's electricity infrastructure sits unused for most of the year because it is built to meet short periods of peak demand.

headline: Spiritus Appoints Former Tesla Engineering Leader Dorian West and Brightmark Project Lead Kip Hensley to Support Commercial Scale-Up
summary: SANTA FE, N.M., March 19, 2026--Spiritus, a climate technology company developing carbon-negative power generation and industrial-scale direct air capture systems, today announced two leadership appointments that deepen the company’s execution bench as it delivers firm, carbon-negative power infrastructure for AI data centers and other power-intensive customers. Kip Hensley has joined as Head of Project Development, and Dorian West has joined Spiritus’s Board of Directors.

Kip Hensley has joined as Head of Project Development, and Dorian West has joined Spiritus’s Board of Directors.

headline: Tesla faces new questions about how cars in self-driving mode handle poor conditions
summary: Federal auto regulators have escalated a probe of Tesla after several of its cars crashed while using its self-driving feature, just as CEO Elon Musk prepares to roll out a new model with no steering wheel or pedals.  The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said in a memo that it was examining nine crashes where the self-driving software failed to quickly alert drivers to take control in fog and other poor conditions because the vehicle's cameras weren't picking out road hazards.  The NHTSA memo signals a regulatory investigation begun in 2024 over poor visibility crashes could now lead to enforcement action, possibly including a recall of 3.2 million Tesla vehicles.

content.title: Tesla faces new questions about how cars in self-driving mode handle poor conditions
content.summary: Federal auto regulators have escalated a probe of Tesla after several of its cars crashed while using its self-driving feature, just as CEO Elon Musk prepares to roll out a new model with no steering wheel or pedals.  The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said in a memo that it was examining nine crashes where the self-driving software failed to quickly alert drivers to take control in fog and other poor conditions because the vehicle's cameras weren't picking out road hazards.  The NHTSA memo signals a regulatory investigation begun in 2024 over poor visibility crashes could now lead to enforcement action, possibly including a recall of 3.2 million Tesla vehicles.

headline: Tesla faces wider probe of self-driving feature as it prepares to sell cars without steering wheels
summary: Federal auto regulators have escalated a probe of Tesla after several of its cars crashed while using its self-driving feature, just as CEO Elon Musk prepares to roll out a new model with no steering wheel or pedals.  The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said in a memo dated March 18 that it was examining nine crashes where the self-driving software failed to quickly alert drivers to take control in fog and other poor conditions because the vehicle's cameras weren't picking out road hazards.  The NHTSA memo signals a regulatory investigation begun in 2024 over poor visibility crashes could now lead to enforcement action, possibly including a recall of 3.2 million Tesla vehicles.

content.title: Tesla faces wider probe of self-driving feature as it prepares to sell cars without steering wheels
content.summary: Federal auto regulators have escalated a probe of Tesla after several of its cars crashed while using its self-driving feature, just as CEO Elon Musk prepares to roll out a new model with no steering wheel or pedals.  The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said in a memo dated March 18 that it was examining nine crashes where the self-driving software failed to quickly alert drivers to take control in fog and other poor conditions because the vehicle's cameras weren't picking out road hazards.  The NHTSA memo signals a regulatory investigation begun in 2024 over poor visibility crashes could now lead to enforcement action, possibly including a recall of 3.2 million Tesla vehicles.

summary: Rivian’s place in Uber’s long‑term robotaxi rollout puts scale, autonomy, and future margins in sharp focus, today, March 19, 2026.

headline: Elon Musk's 'DOGEfather' meme fails to lift memecoin
summary: The world's wealthiest individual, Elon Musk, is well-known for his leadership of Tesla, SpaceX, X (formerly Twitter), and xAI. The tech tycoon has also hailed cryptocurrencies and once used to talk or share posts about Dogecoin (DOGE) quite often. Launched in December 2013, Dogecoin is ...
